chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ وَفَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 111 – Nazool (Arrival) of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> and his<sup>saww</sup> demise

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (223/ 5)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (223/ 5)
text: لَمَّا قُبِضَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) بَاتَ آلُ مُحَمَّدٍ ( عليهم السلام ) بِأَطْوَلِ لَيْلَةٍ حَتَّى ظَنُّوا أَنْ لَا سَمَاءَ تُظِلُّهُمْ وَ لَا أَرْضَ تُقِلُّهُمْ لِأَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَتَرَ الْأَقْرَبِينَ وَ الْأَبْعَدِينَ فِي اللَّهِ

فَبَيْنَا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ أَتَاهُمْ آتٍ لَا يَرَوْنَهُ وَ يَسْمَعُونَ كَلَامَهُ فَقَالَ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَ بَرَكَاتُهُ إِنَّ فِي اللَّهِ عَزَاءً مِنْ كُلِّ مُصِيبَةٍ وَ نَجَاةً مِنْ كُلِّ هَلَكَةٍ وَ دَرَكاً لِمَا فَاتَ

كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ وَ إِنَّما تُوَفَّوْنَ أُجُورَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ فَمَنْ زُحْزِحَ عَنِ النَّارِ وَ أُدْخِلَ الْجَنَّةَ فَقَدْ فازَ وَ مَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا إِلَّا مَتاعُ الْغُرُورِ

إِنَّ اللَّهَ اخْتَارَكُمْ وَ فَضَّلَكُمْ وَ طَهَّرَكُمْ وَ جَعَلَكُمْ أَهْلَ بَيْتِ نَبِيِّهِ وَ اسْتَوْدَعَكُمْ عِلْمَهُ وَ أَوْرَثَكُمْ كِتَابَهُ وَ جَعَلَكُمْ تَابُوتَ عِلْمِهِ وَ عَصَا عِزِّهِ وَ ضَرَبَ لَكُمْ مَثَلًا مِنْ نُورِهِ وَ عَصَمَكُمْ مِنَ الزَّلَلِ وَ آمَنَكُمْ مِنَ الْفِتَنِ فَتَعَزَّوْا بِعَزَاءِ اللَّهِ

فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَمْ يَنْزِعْ مِنْكُمْ رَحْمَتَهُ وَ لَنْ يُزِيلَ عَنْكُمْ نِعْمَتَهُ فَأَنْتُمْ أَهْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ الَّذِينَ بِهِمْ تَمَّتِ النِّعْمَةُ وَ اجْتَمَعَتِ الْفُرْقَةُ وَ ائْتَلَفَتِ الْكَلِمَةُ وَ أَنْتُمْ أَوْلِيَاؤُهُ فَمَنْ تَوَلَّاكُمْ فَازَ وَ مَنْ ظَلَمَ حَقَّكُمْ زَهَقَ

مَوَدَّتُكُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَاجِبَةٌ فِي كِتَابِهِ عَلَى عِبَادِهِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ثُمَّ اللَّهُ عَلَى نَصْرِكُمْ إِذَا يَشَاءُ قَدِيرٌ فَاصْبِرُوا لِعَوَاقِبِ الْأُمُورِ فَإِنَّهَا إِلَى اللَّهِ تَصِيرُ قَدْ قَبَّلَكُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ نَبِيِّهِ وَدِيعَةً وَ اسْتَوْدَعَكُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَمَنْ أَدَّى أَمَانَتَهُ آتَاهُ اللَّهُ صِدْقَهُ

فَأَنْتُمُ الْأَمَانَةُ الْمُسْتَوْدَعَةُ وَ لَكُمُ الْمَوَدَّةُ الْوَاجِبَةُ وَ الطَّاعَةُ الْمَفْرُوضَةُ وَ قَدْ قُبِضَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ قَدْ أَكْمَلَ لَكُمُ الدِّينَ وَ بَيَّنَ لَكُمْ سَبِيلَ الْمَخْرَجِ فَلَمْ يَتْرُكْ لِجَاهِلٍ حُجَّةً فَمَنْ جَهِلَ أَوْ تَجَاهَلَ أَوْ أَنْكَرَ أَوْ نَسِيَ أَوْ تَنَاسَى فَعَلَى اللَّهِ حِسَابُهُ وَ اللَّهُ مِنْ وَرَاءِ حَوَائِجِكُمْ وَ أَسْتَوْدِعُكُمُ اللَّهَ وَ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ
فَسَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) مِمَّنْ أَتَاهُمُ التَّعْزِيَةُ فَقَالَ مِنَ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad Al Ashary, from Moalla Bin Myhammad, from Mansour Bin Al Abbas, from Ali Bin Asbat, from Yaqoub Bin Salim, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> passed away, the Progeny<sup>asws</sup> of Muhammad<sup>saww</sup> spent long nights until they<sup>asws</sup> thought that there is no sky to shade them nor any land below them<sup>asws</sup>, because Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> had held together the near ones and the far ones for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>.

So while they<sup>asws</sup> were in that (situation), a comer came to them. They<sup>asws</sup> were not seeing him but they<sup>asws</sup> were hearing his speech. So he said, ‘The greetings be upon you<sup>asws</sup> the People<sup>asws</sup> of the Household, and the Mercy of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Blessings! In Allah<sup>azwj</sup> there is a consolation from every difficulty and salvation from every destruction, and recovery of whatever is lost.

[3:185] Every self shall taste the death, but rather you shall only be paid fully your Reward on the Day of Judgement; then whoever is removed far away from the Fire and is made to enter the Paradise he indeed has succeeded; and what is the life of the world except for a deceptive pleasure.

Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Chose you all (Imams<sup>asws</sup>), and Preferred you<sup>asws</sup>, and Purified you<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>, and Made you<sup>asws</sup> the People<sup>asws</sup> of the Household of His<sup>azwj</sup> Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, and Entrusted His<sup>azwj</sup> Knowledge to you<sup>asws</sup>, and Made you<sup>asws</sup> to inherit His<sup>azwj</sup> Book, and Made you<sup>asws</sup> a Container of His<sup>azwj</sup> Knowledge, and a Staff of His<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty, And Struck for you<sup>asws</sup> a resemblance from His<sup>azwj</sup> Light and Protected you<sup>asws</sup> from the error, and Secured you<sup>asws</sup> from the strife. Thus, you<sup>asws</sup> were consoled by the Condolences of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>.

So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> did not Remove His<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy from you<sup>asws</sup> and never Declined His<sup>azwj</sup> Bounties from you<sup>asws</sup>. You<sup>asws</sup> are ‘أَهْلُ اللَّهِ’ the People<sup>asws</sup> of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, those by whom<sup>asws</sup> the Bounties are completed, and the parties gather together, and the speeches are harmonised. And you<sup>asws</sup> are the Guardians<sup>asws</sup> of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>. So the one who is in your<sup>asws</sup> Wilayah would succeed and the one who oppressed your<sup>asws</sup> rights would vanish.

(Adopting) your<sup>asws</sup> cordiality is from Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, being an Obligation in His<sup>azwj</sup> Book upon His<sup>azwj</sup> servants who are the Momineen. Then, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> is Able upon Helping you<sup>asws</sup> whenever He<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires to. Therefore, be patient for the consequential ending of the affairs, for these are proceeding to Allah<sup>azwj</sup>. Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has Accepted you<sup>asws</sup> from His<sup>azwj</sup> Prophet<sup>saww</sup> as a depository and Entrusted you<sup>asws</sup> with His<sup>azwj</sup> friends, the Momineen, in the earth. So the one who fulfils his entrustment, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Give him (Rewards for) his truthfulness.

Thus, you<sup>asws</sup> are the Entrusted entrustments, and for you<sup>asws</sup> is the Obligatory cordiality and the necessitated obedience, and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has Captured (the soul of) His<sup>azwj</sup> Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, and has Perfected the Religion for you all and Explained to you the way out (deliverance). Therefore He<sup>azwj</sup> did not leave an argument for the ignorant one. So the one who is ignorant or pretends to be ignorant, or denies, or forgets, or pretends to forget, so his Reckoning is upon Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> is behind (the fulfilment of) all your needs, and I<sup>asws</sup> entrust you all to Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and the greetings be upon you all’.
So I asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, ‘From who does the consolation come to them<sup>asws</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘From Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High’.
en.sarwar: 18. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Sa‘d ibn ‘Abdallah from a group of our people from Ahmad ibn Hilal from ’Umayya ibn Ali al-Qaysi who has said that narrated to me Durust ibn abu Mansur who has said the following. “ I once asked abu al-Hassan (a.s.), ‘Did the Messenger of Allah receive any authority from abu Talib (a.s.) ?” The Imam (a.s.) said, “No, but abu Talib was the trustee of certain (items) that he delivered to the Holy Prophet (s.a.) .” I then asked, “What was the condition of abu Talib?” The Imam (a.s.) Imam said, “ He acknowledged the Holy Prophet (s.a.) and his Divine message. He delivered to him the (Items of) will and died on that day.”
